title: Database migrations can lock, rewrite, or lose data
description: Schema and index changes may lock tables, rewrite rows, break old code, or destroy information even when syntax is valid.
body: Use when migration files or schema diffs appear. Do not execute production migrations. Actions: inspect destructive operations, lock behavior, transaction boundaries, backfill volume, and old/new version compatibility. Validation signals include local migration, dry-run, explain/plan notes, and rollback checkpoints. Risk boundary: drop, rename, type narrowing, or bulk rewrite requires human gate. Use when Production Change Risk Controls work touches repository conventions, toolchain files, changed symbols, validation commands, and runtime output. Do not use this guidance to override a more specific pack, user instruction, or repository contract. Actions: use the fact to constrain the change and test plan; inspect the current files first, keep the change scoped to the owning boundary, and record what evidence changed the decision. Validation signals include package-local checks, focused tests, build output, smoke runs, and command logs. Risk boundary: escalate or stop when the task involves broad rewrites, generated-file drift, stale assumptions, and unvalidated behavior changes, missing owner evidence, or live external state.
